hero.subtitle: A gathering place for shared plates, natural wines, and evenings that linger.

homeStory.title: Where every meal is
homeStory.text: The word مائدة (ma'ida) means "the gathering table" in Arabic - where family and friends come together, glasses clink, plates are shared, and time lingers. That's the spirit we brought from Beirut to Cais do Sodré.

featuredFood.name: Shish Barak
featuredFood.description: Fried minced beef dumplings sautéed in chili paste, served over garlicky yogurt and caramelized onions with roasted almonds.

featuredFood.name: Kebab Pistachio
featuredFood.description: Minced meat with pistachios and house spices, sautéed in pomegranate molasses. Served with hummus.

featuredFood.name: Muhamara
featuredFood.description: Roasted red pepper dip, walnuts, tahini, pomegranate molasses and a hint of chili.

featuredFood.name: Shawarma
featuredFood.description: (beef) with fries, onions and parsley salad, tomato, pickles, tahini dressing and saj bread.

story.label: #FromOurRoots
story.title: Where every meal is
story.text1: Maída is more than a restaurant - it's an invitation. To slow down. To gather around the table. To share something meaningful.
story.text2: The word مائدة (ma'ida) means "the gathering table" in Arabic - where family and friends come together, stories are shared, and no one leaves hungry. That's the spirit we brought from Beirut to Cais do Sodré.

pillars.title: Shareable Plates
pillars.description: Our menu is designed for the table, not the individual. Dishes meant to be passed, tasted, and enjoyed together.

pillars.title: Natural Wines
pillars.description: Small producers, organic vineyards, Mediterranean terroir. Each bottle tells its own story.

pillars.title: Linger Longer
pillars.description: A space for staying-not rushing. For the second glass, the extra mezze, the story that couldn't wait.

drinks.label: From Our Roots
drinks.title: Cocktails with
drinks.tagline: 8 drinks. 8 stories. Different roads that led us here.
drinks.text: Our bar tells the same story as our kitchen. Each cocktail draws from home: rose water, orange blossom, sumac, arak.

about.heroSubtitle: A place where flavors, music, and good company come together. Rooted in tradition, reimagined for today.
about.meaningText1: Inspired by المائدة (al-mā'idah), the beloved tradition of gathering, feasting, and celebrating with loved ones around a big table, Maída brings people together with authentic Mediterranean flavors & drinks, our in-house Saj Manoushe, and dishes that evolve throughout the day - all complemented by a smooth musical experience, elevated by our hi-fi sound system.
about.meaningText2: It's the gathering table. Where family and friends come together, and stories are shared between bites.
about.rootsText1: In Lebanon, meals are never rushed. They're occasions - long gatherings where everyone lingers, glasses clink, and stories grow louder with every round.
about.rootsText2: When we arrived in Lisbon, we discovered something familiar: the Portuguese share this same love for gathering around a big table and turning a meal into an occasion. It felt like home.
about.rootsText3: That's how two worlds became one. Maída is a Mediterranean restaurant with Lebanese soul in the heart of Cais do Sodré - where Portuguese warmth meets Lebanese generosity.
about.foundersAnna: is a culinary and animation director by heart, passionate about food, music, and the creative world. Over the years, she refined her skills in cooking and food presentation, turning every plate into a canvas.
about.foundersAnthony: is an electrical engineer and consultant by trade, fascinated by the world of hospitality, mixology, and creating experiences that linger.
about.foundersStory: After opening The Happy Salad in Lisboa, they shared a dream: to present Lebanese food from a different perspective - not just the familiar classics, but the dishes they grew up eating at home.
about.foundersToday: Today, you'll find them where they belong: at the table with you - making sure every detail is right, every glass is full, and no guest ever leaves without a story to tell.
about.ctaTitle: Find your place at the table

saj.intro: Saj is a traditional Lebanese flatbread cooked on a convex griddle - the same technique used for centuries in the mountains of Lebanon.

tradition.title: A centuries-old tradition
tradition.text: Saj is more than just bread - it's an art form. Stretched thin by hand and cooked in seconds over a hot griddle, it becomes crispy on the outside and soft inside.

atMaida.title: Saj at Maída
atMaida.text: At Maída, we make our saj fresh every day. You can enjoy it as a wrap with our savoury or sweet fillings, or as an accompaniment to our dips and mezze.

coffeeTea.heroSubtitle: From the Middle East to your cup

coffee.title: Coffee
coffee.text: Our coffee is locally roasted in Lisbon, with beans selected from around the world. Try our traditional Lebanese coffee with cardamom.

tea.title: Tea
tea.text: Fresh teas prepared to order - from classic Lebanese mint tea to ginger and lemon blends to warm the soul.
